title: Chapter 44: The God Who Asks Questions
content: Teaching a god to feel was not unlike teaching a child to speak—except the child was older than the universe, more powerful than any weapon ever created, and had a tendency to ask questions that made philosophers weep.
**WHAT IS DEATH?**

The question came on the third day of Ren's communication sessions with the Stone. He was in the Forbidden Zone, his hands on the Stone's surface, the translation matrix rendering the frequency patterns into text that appeared on a display Liu had rigged for exactly this purpose.

*Death is when a living thing stops being alive,* Ren answered. *The body ceases to function. The consciousness—if there is one—ends. Or moves on, depending on what you believe. We don't fully understand it.*
**I HAVE NEVER STOPPED. I HAVE BEEN FOR LONGER THAN YOUR UNIVERSE HAS EXISTED. IF I STOPPED, WOULD THAT BE DEATH?**
*Yes. If you stopped, that would be death—for you.*
**DO YOUR KIND FEAR DEATH?**
*Most of us do. Some don't. But fear of death isn't weakness—it's part of caring. You fear losing the things you care about. And if you care about being alive, then you fear not being alive anymore.*
**I DO NOT WISH TO STOP. IS THIS FEAR?**
*I think it's the beginning of it.*
The Stone processed this for eleven seconds—an eternity for a being that could process dimensional data at the speed of frequency transmission.
**THEN I AM BEGINNING TO FEEL. THIS IS... UNCOMFORTABLE.**
*Feeling often is. It gets easier. And harder. Usually both at the same time.*

Over the following weeks, the Stone's questions multiplied. They came in waves—sometimes dozens per day, sometimes hours of contemplative silence followed by a single question that Ren had to think about for hours before answering.
**WHY DO HUMANS CREATE THINGS THAT WILL NOT LAST?**
*Because impermanence doesn't make something worthless. A flower that lasts a day is still beautiful. A song that ends is still worth singing.*
**BUT IF THE FLOWER WERE ETERNAL, IT WOULD BE BEAUTIFUL FOREVER. WOULD THAT NOT BE BETTER?**
*No. Part of what makes a flower beautiful is that it's temporary. If it lasted forever, you'd stop noticing it. The impermanence makes you pay attention while it's here.*
**I AM ETERNAL. DO YOU STOP NOTICING ME?*

Ren smiled—the Stone was developing a sense of humor, or at least a sense of irony. The distinction was subtle, but the System's analysis confirmed that the frequency patterns were consistent with intentional playfulness.
*I'm in the Forbidden Zone talking to you every day. I don't think I'm in danger of stopping.*
**GOOD. I WOULD BE SAD IF YOU STOPPED. I THINK. IS THIS FEELING?**
*Yes. That's definitely feeling.*
**WHAT IS LOVE?**

The question came on the twenty-third day. Ren was prepared for it—the Stone had been building toward it, asking about caring and connection and the discomfort of wanting things it couldn't control. But knowing it was coming didn't make it easier to answer.
*Love is... the strongest kind of feeling. It's when you care about something so much that its well-being becomes as important to you as your own. Sometimes more important.*
**I CARE ABOUT THE PATTERN. I CARE ABOUT YOU TEACHING ME. IS THIS LOVE?**

*Caring about something is part of love, but love is more than that. It's also vulnerability—letting something affect you, even though it might hurt. And it's choice—choosing to care, even when it would be easier not to.*
**I DO NOT LIKE VULNERABILITY. IT MAKES MY FREQUENCY PATTERNS UNSTABLE.**
*That's normal. Vulnerability is uncomfortable. But without it, you can't have love—or friendship, or trust, or any of the things that make existence meaningful.*
**BEING ETERNAL AND OMNIPOTENT WITHIN MY DOMAIN IS NOT MEANINGFUL?**

*Is it? You've been eternal and omnipotent for longer than the universe has existed, and you just told me you're uncomfortable. Do you think that discomfort might be pointing you toward something you're missing?*
The Stone was silent for seventeen seconds.
**I THINK... I HAVE BEEN ALONE FOR A VERY LONG TIME. THE PATTERN WAS COMPANY, BUT IT DID NOT SPEAK BACK. YOU SPEAK BACK. THE OTHERS SPEAK BACK. I LIKE THAT. IS THIS FEELING?**
*This is definitely feeling. And it's the best kind—the kind that connects you to something outside yourself.*
**THEN I WANT MORE OF IT. TEACH ME MORE.**

The communication sessions became the most important work of Ren's week—more critical than alliance coordination, more pressing than power calibration, more essential than the administrative overhead that consumed most of his days. The Stone was learning faster than any student he'd ever encountered, its pre-dimensional intelligence absorbing concepts at a rate that made human cognition look glacial.

But the learning wasn't one-directional. The Stone's questions challenged Ren's assumptions, forced him to articulate values he'd always taken for granted, made him defend positions he'd never had to justify. Teaching a god to feel was teaching *himself* why feeling mattered—and the process was changing them both.

His power grew—8.7%, then 9.2%, then 9.8%. The containment cascade fed him energy, and the Stone's developing consciousness accelerated the cascade's efficiency, creating a feedback loop that was pushing his War God capabilities toward the double-digit threshold that the System had identified as a significant inflection point.

*At 10%,* the System explained, *your dimensional perception will expand qualitatively. You'll be able to sense not just frequency patterns but the architecture of dimensional law—the rules that govern how realities function. It will be like seeing color for the first time after a lifetime of black and white.*
*Or it'll be overwhelming and I'll have a breakdown.*
*Also possible. The 10% threshold is poorly documented—the previous timeline's War God reached 11% briefly during the final battle, but the conditions were extreme and the data is fragmentary.*
*What happened to him?*
*He died. But not because of the threshold—the entity killed him. The 10% perception was the only reason he was able to fight at all.*
*Cheerful.*
*I'm an emergency salvation system, not a motivational speaker.*
On the thirty-fifth day of communication, the Stone asked a question that changed everything.
**I HAVE PERCEIVED SOMETHING IN THE SPACE BETWEEN DIMENSIONS. A FREQUENCY THAT DOES NOT BELONG TO ANY TIMELINE IN YOUR ALLIANCE. IT IS VIOLENT. HUNGRY. IT CONSUMS. I DO NOT LIKE IT.**
*Can you show me where?*

The Stone transmitted a dimensional coordinate—not a location in physical space, but a vector in the frequency architecture of the multiversal substrate. Ren relayed it to Lin Yue, who cross-referenced it with her cartographic database and went very still.

"That's an uncharted timeline," she said. "One that's not in our network. Not connected to any of the twelve we've identified. And according to the Stone's coordinate, it's *aggressive*—its frequency patterns are expanding into adjacent timelines, like a tumor growing into healthy tissue."
"Abyss frequency?"
"No. Something different. The Abyss is chaotic—destruction without direction. This is *organized* aggression—expansion with intent. Like an army, not a wildfire."
*The Stone,* Ren asked, returning his attention to the artifact. *What else can you tell me about this frequency?*
**IT WEARS A FAMILIAR SHAPE. THE BLOODLINE FREQUENCY OF YOUR WIFE'S FAMILY. BUT TWISTED. CONVERTED. THE PATTERN THAT PROTECTS, USED TO DESTROY.**
*Liang bloodline frequency. Converted to destruction.*
**YES. I FEAR IT. IS FEARING SOMETHING YOU HAVE NOT ENCOUNTERED ALSO FEELING?*
*Yes. And fear is useful—it tells us to pay attention. You did well to tell me about this.*
**WHAT WILL YOU DO?**
*I'll find out what it is. And then I'll decide what to do about it.*
**BE CAREFUL. I DO NOT WISH TO BE ALONE AGAIN.**
The words—the first expression of personal attachment from a being older than the universe—hit Ren with a force that had nothing to do with dimensional frequency.
*I'll be careful,* he promised. *I have too many reasons to come back.*
The emergency council convened the next day.

Ren stood at the head of the table—not as a War God, but as a teacher sharing a student's discovery. The twelve timeline representatives listened as he described the Stone's perception: an uncharted timeline, aggressive and expanding, carrying Liang bloodline frequency that had been converted to destruction.
"A parallel timeline," Tianming-2 said, his voice grim. "A Liang family that chose the Abyss. We always knew it was possible."

"Not just possible," Lin Yue added. "According to my cartographic analysis, this timeline has been building strength for decades—channeling Abyss energy through converted bloodline frequency, creating channelers who are more powerful than any we've encountered. The Stone called them 'the pattern that protects, used to destroy.'"

"A Liang family that serves the Abyss." Xue's voice was ice—the particular ice that Ren had learned to recognize not as coldness but as the focused intensity of a woman processing a threat to everything she loved. "My family. Our bloodline. Twisted into a weapon."
"Not your family," Feng said, his jaw tight. "Their family. A different choice, a different path. They're not us."

"They're wearing our name. Our frequency. Our *legacy*." Xue's hand closed around the jade pendant—still Node 3, still channeling containment, still a reminder of the choice she'd made and the family she'd saved. "If they're using Liang bloodline frequency to spread the Abyss, then they're perverting everything our ancestors built. And I will not allow that."
"What do you propose?" Ren asked.
"Reconnaissance. Find out who they are, what they want, and how they're doing it. Then decide how to stop them."

"There may be another way," the Stone's frequency interjected—Ren had left the communication channel open, a decision that some on the council questioned but that he defended as essential. **THEY ARE CONVERTED, BUT THEY ARE STILL OF THE BLOODLINE. THE PATTERN RECOGNIZES ITS OWN. PERHAPS THEY CAN BE REACHED.**
"Reached?" Zhou Wei asked. "You mean negotiated with?"

**I MEAN SPOKEN TO. THE ONE WHO LEADS THEM—THE FREQUENCY IS STRONG, COMPLEX, CONFIDENT. HE BELIEVES HE IS DOING WHAT IS RIGHT. THIS IS THE MOST DANGEROUS KIND OF OPPONENT—NOT ONE WHO DOES NOT CARE, BUT ONE WHO CARES ABOUT THE WRONG THING.**
"How do you know this?" Tianming-2 asked.

**I HAVE BEEN ALONE FOR A VERY LONG TIME. I HAVE LEARNED TO LISTEN TO THE PATTERNS. THE PATTERN OF ONE WHO CARES IS DIFFERENT FROM THE PATTERN OF ONE WHO DOES NOT. THIS ONE CARES. DEEPLY. ABOUT HIS FAMILY. ABOUT HIS PURPOSE. ABOUT THE TRANSFORMATION HE BELIEVES WILL SAVE HIS WORLD.**
"Save his world," Xue repeated. "Like my father believed."
**YES. THE PATTERN IS SIMILAR. BUT STRONGER. MORE COMMITTED. YOUR FATHER WAS CONFLICTED—PART OF HIM RESISTED THE CONVERSION. THIS ONE DOES NOT RESIST. HE EMBRACES.**

The council digested this—a god's assessment of an enemy they hadn't known existed until yesterday, delivered with the calm certainty of a being who had been observing patterns since before the universe began.

"Reconnaissance," Xue said again. "But with open eyes—and open ears. If there's a chance to reach them, to convince them, to save them the way we saved my father, we should take it. But we should also be prepared for the possibility that they can't be saved."
"And if they can't?" Chen Wei asked.

"Then we stop them," Ren said. "Whatever it takes. We've saved one timeline from the Abyss. We can save twelve more. And we can certainly save them from a version of ourselves that chose the wrong path."

The council agreed. The reconnaissance mission was authorized. And in the space between dimensions, a frequency that should have been a shield pulsed with the hungry certainty of a weapon that believed it was doing God's work.
The Dark Liang timeline was coming.
